title: #17 Monitoring Phase 0 final release tasks
completionCriteria: All tasks in this file are Done or explicitly documented as Blocked with a follow-up owner.
Shared TypeScript and Python sanitizers are covered by focused tests.
Sentry initialization is no-op when DSNs are absent and uses recursive PII scrubbing when enabled.
Public health responses do not expose provider key configuration.
Monitoring setup docs list external services, allowed telemetry fields, and known deferred work.
Focused verification, static checks, and git diff checks pass before commit.
docs/plan/execution-order.md is updated last with the actual implementation result.

tasks.task: Create machine-readable task tracker and updater
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Task states can be updated without hand-editing JSON formatting.

tasks.task: Extract TypeScript sanitizer and connect frontend logger
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Nested headers, cookies, tokens, emails, and prompt-like payloads are redacted or dropped in log payloads.

tasks.task: Extract Python sanitizer and connect backend logger
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Backend structured logs use shared redaction for nested extras and known sensitive patterns.

tasks.task: Add privacy-first Sentry initialization for Next.js and FastAPI
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Replay is disabled, DSN absence is safe, and beforeSend scrubs request, breadcrumb, and exception data.
Readiness status does not expose provider key configured booleans or secret-derived details.

tasks.task: Document UptimeRobot and Sentry production setup
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Docs list monitor targets, allowed telemetry, required env vars, and deferred SSL/heartbeat work.

tasks.task: Run focused verification and fix regressions
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Focused tests and static checks pass or failures are documented with a blocking reason.
Execution order reflects completed Phase 0 monitoring work and deferred follow-up tasks.
